mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of compiling smart contract code?

options.text: To execute the contract on the blockchain
To convert the code into bytecode
To test the contract for errors
To deploy the contract on the blockchain

mcqs.explanation: Compiling smart contract code converts it into a format that can be executed by the blockchain, typically bytecode.

mcqs.question: Which of the following platforms supports smart contract deployment?

options.text: Ethereum
Hyperledger Fabric
Corda
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: Ethereum, Hyperledger Fabric, and Corda are all blockchain platforms that support smart contract deployment.

mcqs.question: What is the role of gas in smart contract deployment?

options.text: To determine the cost of contract execution
To execute the contract on the blockchain
To test the contract for errors
To deploy the contract on the blockchain

mcqs.explanation: Gas is a unit of measurement for the computational effort required to execute a smart contract on the Ethereum blockchain, and is used to determine the cost of contract execution.

mcqs.question: Which of the following tools is used to deploy and manage smart contracts on Ethereum?

options.text: Truffle
Web3.js
Fabric SDK
Corda

mcqs.explanation: Truffle is a suite of tools that allows developers to deploy and manage smart contracts on Ethereum.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between deploying and instantiating a smart contract?

options.text: Deploying involves sending the compiled bytecode to the blockchain, while instantiating involves creating an instance of the contract
Deploying involves creating an instance of the contract, while instantiating involves sending the compiled bytecode to the blockchain
Deploying and instantiating are the same thing
Deploying involves testing the contract, while instantiating involves deploying the contract

mcqs.explanation: Deploying a smart contract involves sending the compiled bytecode to the blockchain, while instantiating a smart contract involves creating an instance of the contract and setting its initial state.

mcqs.question: What is the importance of smart contract security and testing?

options.text: To ensure that contracts function as intended and are free from vulnerabilities
To determine the cost of contract execution
To execute the contract on the blockchain
To deploy the contract on the blockchain

mcqs.explanation: Smart contract security and testing are crucial to ensure that contracts function as intended and are free from vulnerabilities that can be exploited by malicious actors.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)?

options.text: It executes smart contract code on the Ethereum blockchain
It compiles smart contract code into bytecode
It tests smart contracts for errors
It deploys smart contracts on the blockchain

mcqs.explanation: The EVM executes smart contract code on the Ethereum blockchain, providing a decentralized and secure environment for contract execution.

mcqs.question: What is the role of Fabric SDK in smart contract deployment?

options.text: It deploys and manages smart contracts on Ethereum
It deploys and manages smart contracts on Hyperledger Fabric
It tests smart contracts for errors
It compiles smart contract code into bytecode

mcqs.explanation: Fabric SDK is a set of tools that allows developers to deploy and manage smart contracts on Hyperledger Fabric.
